name: Ross Island (Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose Island)
description: Haunting colonial ruins reclaimed by nature, where massive tree roots engulf crumbling British buildings and spotted deer roam freely through the overgrown pathways.
history: Served as the administrative headquarters of the British in the Andaman Islands from 1858 until an earthquake in 1941 forced evacuation.
famousFor: British colonial ruins, free-roaming deer, and Light & Sound show
